markdown.markdown:
::: {.landing-wrap}

::: {.status-pill}
Digital Musicology Project
:::

::: {.hero-screen}
::: {.hero-copy}
::: {.hero-mega}
The Sound of Resonance.
:::

::: {.hero-lead}
A digital musicology project analyzing how video game soundtrack scale
evolved from early hardware-limited compositions to modern cinematic and
orchestral scores.
:::
:::
:::

::: {.overview-panel}
::: {.overview-card}

This project investigates how the structure and scale of video game
soundtracks changed across technological eras. By combining a historical
timeline, a manually constructed dataset, listening examples, and
exploratory analysis, the site demonstrates how technological change
shaped the evolution of video game music.
:::
:::

## Introduction

Video game music has evolved dramatically over time. Early games were shaped by hardware limitations and relied on short looping melodies. Modern games, by contrast, feature larger soundtracks with longer and more varied compositions.

This project investigates how the structure and scale of video game soundtracks changed across technological eras.

## Research Question

How has the scale of video game soundtracks changed across technological eras?

## Project Scope

::: {.overview-grid}
::: {.scope-card}
#### Historical Timeline
Interactive context for major shifts in game music history.
:::

::: {.scope-card}
#### Dataset
A manually constructed soundtrack dataset across multiple eras.
:::

::: {.scope-card}
#### Analysis
Track counts, runtime, and structural soundtrack comparisons.
:::

::: {.scope-card}
#### Listening
Embedded examples that let users hear musical change directly.
:::
:::

## History of Video Game Music

The timeline below highlights key moments in the evolution of video game music, from early electronic loops to modern cinematic scoring.

<iframe
  src="https://cdn.knightlab.com/libs/timeline3/latest/embed/index.html?source=v2%3A2PACX-1vSriMzVf35i-a6C6ICckzYUZZ7CMFI9_KO1pHxIlgXtCi2f-7xcDrE-UWyzM-jGd3z7d8O3ND4ysOlg&font=Default&lang=en&initial_zoom=2&width=100%25&height=650"
  width="100%"
  height="750"
  webkitallowfullscreen
  mozallowfullscreen
  allowfullscreen
  frameborder="0"></iframe>

## Why This Matters

Video game music has grown from a technical necessity into a major component of interactive storytelling. By comparing soundtrack structure across eras, this project shows how technological constraints and innovations shaped the development of video game composition.

:::
